Header.Name: Letter-v2
Header.Description: Generic elements for letter-metadata description, compliant with the CIDOC-CRM-based ontology CER (https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#).

Component.name: Letter-v2
Component.Documentation: A single message from a sender to one or more organizations or people.

Element.name: project
Element.Documentation: For internal use to assign a label to a specific project.

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type of letter identifier (e.g., Shelfmark, project local

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark). If you chose "Other" in the Identifier type, enter here in parenthesis where the identifier comes from, for example: N011111 (Nodegoat).

Element.name: identifierUrl
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here.

Element.name: abstract
Element.Documentation: An abstract is a short description that summarizes a CreativeWork.

Element.name: to
Element.Documentation: Start typing and select the person from the other Editor. If the person doesn't exist yet, create it in the person Editor first.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: DateSent
Component.Documentation: "This property associates an instance of CER12 Correspondence Action or CER15 Editing or CER24 Co-Presence with one instance of CER18 Time-Span during which the Correspondence Action (CER12) (like Sending (CER13) or Receiving (CER14)) or the Editing process or the inferred Co-Presence of two historical actors was ongoing. The associated instance of CER18 Time-Span is understood as the real time-span during which the phenomena making up the temporal entity instance were active."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the date as observed in the source (as written or transcribed, or extracted).

Component.name: DateNormalized
Component.Documentation: For complete dates or date ranges use: YYYY-MM-DD. Example: 2025-07-17 (17 July 2025). For uncertain dates or date ranges, use these rules: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions from the link above

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range), follow the instructions from the link above

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: Add any clarifying notes related to the dates.

Component.name: PlaceSent
Component.Documentation: "This property describes the Place (CER19) where a Correspondence Action (CER12) happened, meaning the sending and receiving place, as well as the where the Co-Presence (CER24) of two histociral actors, inferred through reasoning-processes, occured."

Element.name: to
Element.Documentation: Start typing and select the place from the other Editor. If the place doesn't exist yet, create it in the place Editor first.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: Receiving
Component.Documentation: "This class comprises the receiving process of correspondence items involving at least two persons. It has been adapted from the TEI standard for correspondences, specifically drawing from the correspAction element. The aim of this class is to supply information concerning the receipt of a message."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: DateReceived
Component.Documentation: For complete dates or date ranges use: YYYY-MM-DD. Example: 2025-07-17 (17 July 2025). For uncertain dates or date ranges, use these rules: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Component.name: DateNormalized
Component.Documentation: For complete dates or date ranges use: YYYY-MM-DD. Example: 2025-07-17 (17 July 2025). For uncertain dates or date ranges, use these rules: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions from the link above

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range), follow the instructions from the link above

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: Add any clarifying notes related to the dates.

Component.name: PlaceReceived
Component.Documentation: "This class is used to refer to places involved in a Correspondence Action (CER12 Correspondence Action) and places which are mentioned in a letter text (CER4 Index Information). They may have an additional authority record like geo names (CER16 Authority Record) but this is not mandatory. Places are not used to give more information about the provenance of a letter, as this is not within the ontology’s scope."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: to
Element.Documentation: Start typing and select the person from the other Editor. If the person doesn't exist yet, create it in the person Editor first.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: providerSource
Element.Documentation: the name of the repository or project (e.g., "correspSearch", "Kalliope", "Deutsches Textarchiv")

Element.name: noteType
Element.Documentation: Add any specific types of notes to the controlled vocabulary.
